title: 10 best herbal tinctures for oily face
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for oily face are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are used to treat and manage oily skin. They are a natural and effective alternative to chemical-based skincare products. Examples of such herbs include witch hazel, yarrow, and burdock root. Witch hazel, for instance, contains tannins that help to reduce oil production and tighten pores, while yarrow has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe irritated skin. Burdock root, on the other hand, is known for its ability to detoxify and purify the skin. By using herbal tinctures, individuals can improve their lives by reducing the appearance of oily skin, preventing acne breakouts, and achieving a more balanced and healthy complexion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tincture helps with oily face because it has antibacterial and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation. Sage contains tannins and volatile oils, such as camphor and cineole, which can help to tighten pores and reduce the appearance of oily skin. Additionally, sage has been shown to have antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. By using sage tincture as a toner or adding it to a facial mask, you can help to balance your skin's natural oil production and promote a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Sage tincture's antiseptic properties help prevent bacteria growth on oily skin, reducing the risk of acne and skin infections. This is due to the presence of active constituents like camphor, tannic acid, and volatile oils.

Astringent: Its astringent properties help tighten and tone oily skin, reducing the appearance of pores and controlling excess sebum production. This is attributed to the tannins and flavonoids present in sage.

Anti-inflammatory: Sage tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness, making it beneficial for those with acne or inflamed skin. This is due to the rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids found in sage.

Sebum-regulating: Sage tincture helps balance sebum production, preventing excessive oiliness and promoting a healthier skin appearance. This is attributed to the presence of ursolic acid and oleanolic acid.

Antioxidant: Sage tincture's antioxidant properties help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health. This is due to the high levels of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and vitamin A found in sage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage leaves help control sebum production and soothe irritated skin.
Flowers: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, sage flowers can gently regulate oil secretion and provide calming effects on problematic, oily skin.
Stems: With astringent properties, sage stems help tighten pores, reduce excess oil, and promote a clearer, more balanced complexion.
Seeds: Abundant in antioxidants and fatty acids, sage seeds support skin health by nourishing, protecting, and rejuvenating oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when essential oils are most concentrated for maximum potency.
Clean and chop 10 grams of dried leaves or 20-25 grams of fresh leaves into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 150-200 milliliters of high-proof grain alcohol or vodka in a clean glass jar, filling it to the top.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14-30 days, shaking the jar daily, allowing the plant compounds to infuse into the solvent.
After the infusion period, strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tincture helps with oily face because it has antibacterial and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production and reduce excess oil on the skin. The rosemary herb contains carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe skin irritation and reduce redness. Additionally, rosemary tincture can help tone and tighten pores, preventing the buildup of dirt and bacteria that can lead to acne and other skin problems. By using rosemary tincture as a natural remedy, you can help maintain a clear and balanced complexion without relying on harsh chemical treatments.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Rosemary tincture's antimicrobial properties help control the growth of acne-causing bacteria on the skin, important for oily face as it can help reduce breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property include camphor, cineol, and bornyl acetate.

Astringent: Rosemary tincture's astringent properties help tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, important for oily face as it can help minimize the appearance of large pores and shine. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and volatile oils.

Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, important for oily face as it can help soothe irritated skin. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and carnosol.

Antioxidant: Rosemary tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, important for oily face as it can help prevent premature aging. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and carnosol.

Circulation-boosting: Rosemary tincture's circulation-boosting properties help increase blood flow to the skin, important for oily face as it can help promote cell turnover and improve skin texture. The active constituents responsible for this property include 1,8-cineole and camphor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain volatile oils and antioxidants that help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation, making them ideal for treating oily face.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, promoting skin health and reducing excess oil production.
Stems: Contain similar medicinal properties as leaves, and can be used interchangeably in tincture preparation.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, which help balance sebum production and soothe irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosemary leaves according to sustainable foraging practices.
Cleanly chop the rosemary leaves into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place to allow extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding solids and sediment.
Store the final rosemary tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tincture helps with oily face because it has antibacterial and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production and reduce the appearance of large pores. Thyme contains a compound called thymol, which has been shown to have antimicrobial effects against Propionibacterium acnes, the bacteria responsible for acne. Additionally, thyme's astringent properties can help tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of shine and providing a more matte finish. By using thyme tincture as a natural and herbal remedy, individuals with oily skin may find that their complexion becomes more balanced and clear over time.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Thyme tincture's antimicrobial properties help control the growth of acne-causing bacteria on the skin, important for oily face as it can reduce the risk of breakouts. This is due to the presence of thymol and carvacrol.

Astringent: Thyme tincture has astringent properties that help to reduce oiliness and shrink pores, making it important for oily face as it can help regulate sebum production. The astringent effect is attributed to the tannins present in thyme.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne, making it important for oily face as it can help alleviate irritation and promote healing. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: Thyme tincture's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, important for oily face as it can help prevent premature aging. This is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic compounds.

Circulation-boosting: Thyme tincture helps to improve blood flow to the skin, important for oily face as it can help promote the delivery of nutrients and oxygen to the skin, and support the removal of toxins. This is due to the presence of essential oils such as thymol and carvacrol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for oily face due to their antibacterial and sebum-regulating properties.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are rich in essential oils, making them ideal for treating acne-prone skin and balancing sebum production.
Stems: Thyme stems contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and control excess oil on the face when used in tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems in the early morning or late afternoon to ensure optimal potency.
Cleanly chop 100g of harvested material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar leaving about an inch at the top for liquid.
Pour 250ml of high-proof vodka over the chopped thyme material in the jar making sure all parts are completely submerged.
Secure the lid on the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids and label the tincture with date of preparation dosage and storage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tincture helps with oily face because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation in the skin. Oregano is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote skin health. Additionally, oregano tincture can help reduce the appearance of pores and improve the overall texture and tone of the skin, leaving it looking clearer and more balanced. Its astringent properties can help tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of shine and providing a matte finish. Oregano tincture can be a natural and effective solution for those looking to manage oily skin and achieve a clearer, more balanced complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Oregano tincture's antimicrobial properties help control the growth of acne-causing bacteria on the skin, important for oily face as it can help prevent breakouts. This is due to the presence of carvacrol and thymol.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, important for oily face as it can help soothe irritated skin. This is attributed to the rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid.

Astringent: Oregano tincture's astringent properties help to balance sebum production and tighten pores, important for oily face as it can help reduce excess oil and minimize the appearance of pores. This is due to the tannins present.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of oregano tincture help protect the skin from free radical damage, important for oily face as it can help prevent premature aging and promote skin health. This is attributed to the high levels of vitamin E and rosmarinic acid.

Wound healing: Oregano tincture's wound healing properties can help to speed up the healing process of acne lesions, important for oily face as it can help reduce the risk of scarring. This is due to the presence of Beta-caryophyllene and terpinene-4-ol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the oregano plant are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for oily face due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce excess oil and combat acne.
Flowers: The flowers of the oregano plant contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and reduce the appearance of oily skin.
Seeds: The seeds of the oregano plant are rich in essential oils and fatty acids, which can help moisturize and soothe irritated skin, reducing the appearance of oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality oregano leaves at peak potency during early morning hours.
Clean and dry the harvested oregano leaves by gently blowing off excess moisture.
Pack the dried oregano leaves in a clean glass jar leaving about one inch of headspace.
Fill the remaining space with a 40% vodka solution at room temperature for four weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tincture helps with oily face because it contains menthol, a natural compound that has cooling and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help to reduce inflammation and redness in the skin, as well as provide a cooling sensation that can be soothing to oily skin. Additionally, peppermint tincture has antibacterial and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce the presence of acne-causing bacteria on the skin. This can help to prevent the formation of new pimples and blackheads, and may also help to reduce the appearance of existing blemishes. Furthermore, peppermint tincture can help to regulate sebum production, which can help to reduce the amount of oil on the skin and prevent clogged pores. This can lead to a decrease in the appearance of oily skin and a more balanced, healthy complexion.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Peppermint tincture's antiseptic properties help to kill bacteria on the skin, preventing acne and inflammation. This is important for oily face as excess oil can create a breeding ground for bacteria, leading to breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property include menthol and menthone.

Astringent: Peppermint tincture has astringent properties that help to reduce excess oil and tighten pores, preventing the buildup of dirt and debris. This is important for oily face as excess oil can lead to clogged pores, causing blackheads and whiteheads. The active constituents that contribute to this property include tannins and volatile oils.

Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and other skin conditions. This is important for oily face as inflammation can exacerbate the appearance of pores and lead to scarring. The active constituents that provide this property include rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Analgesic: Peppermint tincture has analgesic properties that help to relieve pain and discomfort associated with acne and other skin conditions. This is important for oily face as inflamed pimples and blackheads can be painful and uncomfortable. The active constituents responsible for this property include menthol and menthone.

Circulation-boosting: Peppermint tincture has circulation-boosting properties that help to increase blood flow to the skin, promoting healing and rejuvenation. This is important for oily face as increased circulation can help to reduce the appearance of pores and improve overall skin texture. The active constituents that contribute to this property include menthol and menthone.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain menthol and antiseptic properties, helping to control oily face and preventing acne.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils, flowers help to balance sebum production and soothe irritated skin.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, seeds can help to nourish and moisturize oily skin while reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves mentha x piperita with scissors trim stems to 2-3 inches for optimal essential oil content.
Rinse the harvested peppermint leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned peppermint leaves into small pieces and combine with 60% ethanol vodka in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least 4-6 weeks allowing the mixture to macerate and extract tannins.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding solids and labeling tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with oily face because it has antiseptic and astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation. Its antioxidant properties can also protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to excess oil production. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have a calming effect on the skin, which can help reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin. By using a lemon balm tincture, you can help maintain a balanced and clear complexion, without the risk of over-drying or irritating your skin.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Lemon balm tincture's antiseptic properties help prevent acne-causing bacteria on oily skin. The active constituents responsible for this property are mainly the terpenes, such as citronellal and geraniol.

Astringent: Its astringent properties help tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, which is important for oily face as it helps regulate sebum production and improve skin texture. The rosmarinic and ursolic acids in lemon balm contribute to its astringent qualities.

Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness, which is beneficial for oily face as it helps alleviate inflammation associated with acne or other skin irritations. The rosmarinic and eugenolic acids in lemon balm are responsible for its anti-inflammatory effects.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of lemon balm tincture help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier skin. This is important for oily face as it helps maintain skin health and reduce signs of aging. The primary antioxidants in lemon balm are rosmarinic and caffeic acids.

Calming: Lemon balm tincture's calming properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to excessive sebum production. This is important for oily face as it helps manage the root causes of oiliness and improves overall skin health. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and citral.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for oily face due to their astringent and toning properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain essential oils that help regulate sebum production, making them useful for treating oily skin.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm are rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage and improve its overall health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted source.
Chop or crush the harvested leaves into small pieces.
Combine chopped leaves with 60-80% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with oily face because it contains anti-inflammatory and sebum-regulating properties. These properties can help to reduce excess oil production, unclog pores, and soothe irritated skin. Additionally, lavender has natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent acne breakouts and promote overall skin health. By using a lavender tincture as part of a skincare routine, individuals with oily skin may be able to achieve a more balanced and clear complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: English lavender tincture helps control oily face by inhibiting the growth of acne-causing bacteria due to its high content of compounds like linalool and eucalyptol.
Sebum regulation: Its ability to balance sebum production reduces excess oil on the face, thanks to the presence of tannins and phytosterols.

Anti-inflammatory: Lavender tincture soothes inflamed skin and reduces redness and irritation, which are common in oily, acne-prone skin, owing to its active components like linalyl acetate and flavonoids.

Wound healing: The tincture promotes skin healing and tissue regeneration, which is beneficial for treating acne scars and blemishes on oily skin, due to the synergistic effects of rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.
Calming and soothing: Lavender tincture helps alleviate stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate sebum production and skin conditions, through its key constituents, linalool and lavandulol.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender flowers help regulate sebum production and soothe irritated skin.
Leaves: Containing therapeutic properties similar to flowers, lavender leaves can help reduce redness and combat acne-causing bacteria in oily face tinctures.
Buds: High in essential oils, lavender buds provide potent skin-balancing and calming benefits, making them ideal for treating oily and problematic skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers during peak blooming season ensuring optimal potency.
Clean the harvested flowers by gently rinsing with cool filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned flowers into small pieces and macerate in a 50-50 vodka and water solution for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a glass bottle discarding solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place at room temperature for up to 2 years with occasional shaking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with oily face because it contains hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These compounds can help regulate sebum production, reducing the excess oil that can lead to acne and other skin issues. Additionally, St. John's wort has been shown to have astringent properties, which can help tighten pores and give the skin a more matte appearance. Its soothing effects can also help calm inflamed skin and reduce redness, providing a more balanced and clear complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: St. John's wort tincture has antibacterial properties that help control the growth of acne-causing bacteria on the oily face, preventing breakouts. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin and hyperforin.

Anti-inflammatory: The tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by excess oil and acne, promoting a calmer and more balanced complexion. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Astringent: St. John's wort tincture has astringent properties that help tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, preventing a shiny and greasy appearance. The active constituents responsible for this property are tannins present in the tincture.

Antioxidant: The tincture contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier and more radiant skin. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Wound healing: St. John's wort tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can help reduce the appearance of acne scars and promote a smoother, more even skin tone. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils found in the tincture.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, that have anti-inflammatory and sebum-regulating properties, making them effective for treating oily skin.
Leaves: Contain similar active compounds as flowers, helping to reduce excess oil production, soothe skin irritations, and provide astringent effects.
Buds: High in antioxidants, which help protect the skin from damage, and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and soothe oily, acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall.
Clean and chop the plant material to ensure uniform size for efficient extraction.
Combine 100-200g of chopped plant material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow the solubles to dissolve and combine.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with oily face because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help regulate sebum production and soothe irritated skin. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and carotenoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve overall skin health. Additionally, pot marigold tincture has astringent properties that can help tighten pores and reduce the appearance of oily skin. Its soothing and calming effects can also help alleviate redness and irritation associated with acne and other skin conditions. Overall, pot marigold tincture can help balance and restore the natural moisture of the skin, leaving it looking clearer and healthier.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Pot marigold tincture's antibacterial properties help control oily face by reducing the growth of acne-causing bacteria on the skin. This is due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help soothe inflamed skin and reduce redness, which are common issues with oily face. The active constituents responsible for this are mainly triterpenoids and sterols.

Astringent: Pot marigold tincture has astringent properties that help tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of pores and controlling excess oil production. This is attributed to the tannins present in the tincture.

Wound healing: The wound healing properties of pot marigold tincture can help speed up the recovery of acne lesions and reduce the risk of scarring. This is due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids, carotenoids, and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tincture help protect the skin from free radical damage, promoting healthier and younger-looking skin. This is attributed to the high levels of carotenoids, flavonoids, and vitamin C found in the tincture.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help control sebum production and soothe oily skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain high levels of chlorophyll, vitamins, and minerals that help regulate oil production and have a calming effect on oily and acne-prone skin.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids and antioxidants that help balance sebum production, reduce inflammation, and nourish oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers at peak potency for optimal medicinal properties.
Clean and chop the harvested flowers into smaller pieces to facilitate soaking in menstruum.
Combine one part chopped calendula officinalis with three parts 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle for storage and preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with oily face because it has natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help regulate sebum production, reducing the excess oil that can lead to acne and other skin issues. Additionally, yarrow tincture has been shown to have antibacterial effects, which can further support clear and healthy skin. The herb's ability to tighten pores and soothe irritation also contributes to its effectiveness in managing oily skin. Overall, yarrow tincture offers a natural and holistic approach to addressing the root causes of oily skin, promoting a balanced and radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property helps tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, thanks to its tannin content.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory property of yarrow tincture soothes irritated skin and reduces redness, due to the presence of flavonoids.
Antibacterial: Yarrow tincture's antibacterial property helps control acne-causing bacteria, as it contains volatile oils like azulene.
Antiseborrheic: The antiseborrheic property of yarrow tincture helps regulate sebum production, reducing greasiness on the face, attributed to its lactone content.
Wound-healing: Yarrow tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which helps scars and blemishes fade, owing to its plant sterols and sesquiterpene lactones.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that help regulate sebum production in oily skin.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, leaves can help reduce excess oil and soothe irritation.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids and plant sterols, seeds can help balance sebum production and maintain skin's natural barrier.
Stems: Contain tannins that can help tone and tighten oily skin, reducing the appearance of pores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried aerial parts of achillea millefolium, measuring one part by weight for every four parts of menstruum.
Combine the plant material with 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar, filling it to about three-quarters capacity.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for at least two weeks, allowing for maceration.
After the desired maceration time has passed, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, squeezing out as much plant essence as possible.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool place for at least six months before use, allowing for maturation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily face with herbal tinctures is by using astringent herbs like witch hazel or yarrow. These herbs help to tighten the skin, reduce oil production, and minimize the appearance of pores. Another effective herb is calendula, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and heal any existing blemishes. To use, simply dilute the tincture with water and apply it to your face using a cotton ball. Use daily for best results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily face that are treated with herbal tinctures include acne, blackheads, and skin inflammation. For acne, herbal tinctures such as echinacea and goldenseal can be used for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Burdock root and dandelion tinctures can help regulate oil production and improve skin texture. For blackheads, calendula and tea tree oil tinctures can be used for their antiseptic and astringent properties. Additionally, milk thistle and licorice root tinctures can help with skin inflammation and redness. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatment.
